skill.name: obsidian-bases
skill.description: Create and edit Obsidian Bases files with valid YAML views, filters, formulas, summaries, and property settings for Obsidian vaults.
skill.summary: Build structured Obsidian Bases views for notes, tasks, reading lists, projects, and daily indexes.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, filesystem, sensitive key, weak crypto, and heuristic risks. Manual review found these are Markdown examples, inline code, Obsidian formula references, and official documentation links, with no executable script, prompt injection, credential handling, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Command Execution Findings
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, YAML fences, and Obsidian formula examples. The file is documentation and contains no runnable shell, Ruby, or script file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown prose, tables, or fenced YAML examples. No executable code path or shell invocation exists in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Network Findings
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are reference links to Obsidian help pages. The skill does not fetch these URLs or send data to any network endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a References section. There is no network API call or instruction to transmit vault content.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Filesystem, Credential, And Crypto Findings
low_findings.description: The scanner matched Obsidian file metadata, a date regex, object helper names, and table text. These are documentation entries, not filesystem access, certificate handling, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines define Obsidian property names and formula helpers. No certificate files, private keys, hash functions, or direct filesystem reads are present.

content.value_statement: Obsidian Bases can be hard to write correctly when filters, formulas, and summaries interact. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce valid .base structures for organized vault views.
actual_capabilities: Creates Obsidian .base files with table, card, list, and map views.
Defines global and view-specific filters using Obsidian Bases syntax.
Adds formulas for dates, numbers, strings, links, files, lists, and objects.
Configures property display names, formula labels, grouping, limits, and summaries.
Provides complete patterns for task trackers, reading lists, project notes, and daily indexes.
limitations: It documents Obsidian Bases syntax but does not validate files inside Obsidian.
Map views require suitable location properties and the Obsidian Maps community plugin.
It cannot inspect a vault unless the user provides file structure or note metadata.
Generated formulas may need testing against the installed Obsidian version.

use_cases.title: Create a Task Dashboard
use_cases.description: Build a .base file that groups active, overdue, and completed tasks with useful summaries.

use_cases.title: Organize Research Notes
use_cases.description: Create filtered views for articles, books, authors, reading status, and source metadata.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project Views
use_cases.description: Generate consistent project tables with status grouping, last updated formulas, and link counts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Base
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one clear view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian .base file for notes tagged project. Show file name, status, due date, and priority in a table view.

prompt_templates.title: Add Filters and Groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a task tracker base with active and completed views. Filter out archived notes and group active tasks by status.

prompt_templates.title: Design Formula Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Create formulas for days until due, overdue status, and reading time. Add readable display names and useful summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Base
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced editing of a provided .base file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing Obsidian Base and improve its filters, formulas, summaries, and view order while keeping valid YAML.

output_examples.input: Create a base for project notes in the Projects folder.
output_examples.output: A project dashboard with a table view, status grouping, last updated formula, link count formula, and average link summary.

output_examples.input: Make a reading list for books and articles.
output_examples.output: A reading base with card and table views, filters for book and article tags, status icons, authors, pages, and estimated reading time.

output_examples.input: Index daily notes from a date-based folder.
output_examples.output: A recent notes view limited to daily note filenames, with day of week, estimated word count, and modified time columns.

best_practices: Ask for the vault property names before generating formulas and filters.
Use single quotes around formulas that contain double quotes.
Test complex formulas in Obsidian after creating or editing the .base file.
anti_patterns: Do not assume property names such as status, due, or priority exist in every vault.
Do not create map views unless latitude and longitude data are available.
Do not mix global filters and view filters without explaining their combined effect.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates and edits Obsidian Bases .base files that define dynamic views of notes.

faq.question: Does it run code or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation for generating YAML-based Obsidian Base files.

faq.question: Can it create card views and table views?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers table, card, list, and map view structures.

faq.question: Can it write formulas?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents formulas for dates, numbers, strings, files, links, lists, and objects.

faq.question: Does it need access to my Obsidian vault?
faq.answer: No direct access is required. Better results need the property names and folders you use.

faq.question: Can it guarantee Obsidian will accept every generated file?
faq.answer: No. Complex formulas should be tested in Obsidian because plugin and app versions may differ.
